title: Daily Market Digest: May 19, 2026
subtitle: Market closes mixed as tech lags amid inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions, energy gains.

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Close Mixed Amid Geopolitical Unrest and Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. equity markets concluded Tuesday's trading session with a mixed performance. The Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 dipped, largely influenced by surging oil prices, elevated Treasury yields, and a notable retreat in technology shares. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, managed a modest gain, buoyed by strength in energy and industrial sectors. Investors are currently navigating persistent inflation risks, concerns over the Iran war, and the implications of higher bond yields, which continue to drive sector rotations. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Agilysys Surges on Strong Q4 and Upbeat Outlook
items.summary: Shares of Agilysys jumped significantly after the company reported stronger-than-expected Q4 results and issued an upbeat FY2027 guidance, with adjusted EPS of $0.63 and revenue of $82.9M, both surpassing Wall Street estimates.

items.headline: Dominion Rallies on Acquisition News
items.summary: Dominion shares rallied following the announcement that NextEra Energy agreed to acquire the utility in an all-stock deal, poised to create the world's largest regulated electric utility by market value.

items.headline: Boston Scientific Climbs on Share Buyback Program
items.summary: Boston Scientific saw its stock climb after the company announced plans to spend $2 billion of its previously announced $5 billion stock buyback program by the end of June, a move that typically boosts per-share earnings.

items.headline: Regeneron Drops on Disappointing Drug Trial Results
items.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals experienced a significant drop after reporting discouraging data from a trial of its melanoma treatment drug, failing to meet its main goal.

items.headline: Seagate Technology Falls on AI Demand Concerns
items.summary: Seagate Technology's stock dropped after its CEO commented that the company might struggle to keep up with the surging demand for AI-related storage, raising questions about future supply.

items.headline: Micron Technology Declines Amid Broader Tech Selloff
items.summary: Micron Technology's shares fell, contributing to a broader decline in memory chip and other AI-related stocks as the technology sector faced pressure from rising oil prices and Treasury yields.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh to be Sworn in as New Fed Chair Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Kevin Warsh is set to be sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chair on Friday by President Trump, replacing Jerome Powell. His appointment comes at a time when Fed officials are signaling patience on rate cuts, with persistent inflation concerns remaining a key focus, and market expectations for a rate hike in 2026 increasing. Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller also participated in a policy panel today.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Shows Housing Market Improvement, Persistent Inflation
items.summary: The US NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index rose to 37 in May 2026 from 34 in April, exceeding forecasts of 35, indicating some resilience in the housing sector. Meanwhile, the US Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased 3.8% year-over-year in April, the highest reading since May 2023, largely driven by elevated oil and gasoline prices, underscoring ongoing inflation pressures.

items.headline: Home Depot Reports Q1 Earnings; Nvidia Anticipated Later This Week
items.summary: Home Depot ($HD) is reporting its fiscal Q1 FY27 earnings today before the market open, with analysts anticipating EPS near $3.41 on revenues around $41.5 billion. This report is being closely watched for insights into housing and consumer spending trends. Additionally, Nvidia's ($NVDA) highly anticipated earnings report is expected on Wednesday, with Target ($TGT) and Walmart ($WMT) also scheduled to release their quarterly results later this week.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads as Tech Lags Amid AI Boom Divergence
items.summary: The energy sector demonstrated leadership today, propelled by elevated crude oil prices which remained near $110 per barrel due to ongoing geopolitical tensions related to the Iran war. Conversely, the technology sector continued to lag, particularly software stocks, as investors weighed fears of AI disruption against the surging performance of hardware, semiconductor, and memory firms benefiting from the AI boom. This has created a significant performance gap within the tech sector itself.
